sourceMovie.title: Alpha
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Alpha works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: King Kong
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Alpha clicked for you, King Kong (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Fox and the Hound
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Alpha clicked for you, The Fox and the Hound (1981) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Alpha?
faq.answer: Try King Kong, The Fox and the Hound, Mowgli: Legend of the Jungle—each shares DNA with Alpha while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Alpha?
faq.answer: Alpha (2018) is best described as Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Alpha worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
